name: Architecture College Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Handles signup for students, teachers, and admins, JWT login, and authenticated profile access.

# curriculum-service
Academic catalog: careers, areas, professional lines, obligatory and elective subjects, and subject-series relationships.

# enrollment-service
Group and student enrollments, enrollment lock rules, and Mongo-backed bulk preload jobs alongside Postgres transactions.

# grade-service
Captures grades, validation/authorization flows, teacher grading consoles, and academic history projections.

# schedule-service
Class groups (obligatory/elective): create, query, attach teachers, adjust capacity, and emit scheduling-related integration events over RabbitMQ.

# student-service
Student master data: CRUD, lookups, professional line assignment, semester progression, and RabbitMQ integration for domain events.

# teacher-service
Faculty lifecycle: create and delete teachers, validate account numbers, batch lookups, and RabbitMQ integration. JWT validation aligned with account-service.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.

# curriculum-service
Curriculum data is hierarchical (careers, areas, subjects) and must stay normalized, version-friendly, and easy for enrollment and scheduling to consume.

# enrollment-service
Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.

# grade-service
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.

# schedule-service
Timetabling ties classrooms, teachers, and enrollment capacity; updates must stay consistent and visible to enrollment and faculty workflows.

# student-service
Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.

# teacher-service
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.
problemList: Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.

Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.

Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.

decisions.title: Spring Cloud Netflix (Eureka + Config)
decisions.problem: Multiple JVM services need discovery and consistent configuration.
decisions.solution: Use Eureka registration and Spring Cloud Config with config-data YAML.
decisions.outcome: Operational parity between local Compose and target deployment.
alternatives: Consul for discovery and KV config

decisions.title: Relational model for curriculum graph
decisions.problem: Careers, areas, and subjects need constraints and joins for integrity.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL + JPA entities with explicit relationships.
decisions.outcome: Enrollment and scheduling can trust foreign keys and uniqueness in curriculum_db.
alternatives: Document store for entire catalog tree

decisions.title: Postgres + Mongo in one service
decisions.problem: Enrollments need ACID; bulk preload status needs flexible schema.
decisions.solution: JPA for enrollments; Mongo templates/repositories for process documents.
decisions.outcome: Operators get progress APIs without bloating relational tables.
alternatives: Single Postgres JSONB column for job state

decisions.title: Dual store
decisions.problem: Some reads suit documents while grades stay relational.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL for grades; Mongo URI configured for grade_db projections.
decisions.outcome: Flexibility without giving up transactional grade rows.
alternatives: Postgres-only JSON columns for all projections
Event sourcing with separate read stack

decisions.title: Netflix Eureka + Config
decisions.problem: Platform already standardizes on Spring Cloud Netflix.
decisions.solution: Reuse Eureka and Config Server for discovery and YAML.
decisions.outcome: Operational consistency across college microservices.

decisions.title: Separate finder base path
decisions.problem: Read-heavy schedule queries should not compete with transactional endpoints.
decisions.solution: Expose /v1/api/finder/groups for lookups and slices.
decisions.outcome: Clearer caching and scaling story for reads.

decisions.title: Command/query controllers
decisions.problem: Write models differ from read traffic patterns.
decisions.solution: StudentCommandController vs StudentQueryController.
decisions.outcome: Clearer evolution of validation and query optimization.

decisions.title: Split command and query base paths
decisions.problem: Legacy read API used /teachers without version prefix.
decisions.solution: TeacherCommandController under /v1/api/teachers; TeacherQueryController under /teachers.
decisions.outcome: Backward compatibility internally; gateway should unify externally.
alternatives: Single controller with explicit versioning headers

name: Bank API
description: Minimalist modular-monolith banking REST API — IAM, double-entry accounts, transfers with idempotency, loans, append-only audit, and async notifications. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis, and Kafka consumed from a cloud broker; observability stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki) runs on EC2.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Learning banking domains without microservice overhead
problemStatement.problemDescription: Building a credible banking backend requires double-entry ledgers, idempotent payments, loan amortization, audit trails, and secure auth — but a full microservice mesh is heavy for a portfolio or teaching project. Teams also need a path from local Docker to a real cloud deploy without rewriting the domain model.
problemList: Monolithic tutorials skip idempotency, ledger integrity, and append-only audit
JWT + RBAC + refresh rotation is often bolted on without Redis-backed revocation
Cross-module side effects (transfers → ledger → notifications) are hard to test in isolation
Local dev stacks differ from production (H2 vs PostgreSQL, in-process events vs Kafka)
Observability (metrics, structured logs, audit files) is rarely wired end-to-end

solution.solutionTitle: Modular monolith with clean boundaries and AWS-ready deploy

solutionList.title: Domain modules with hexagonal layering
solutionList.description: Nine Maven modules (shared, iam, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications, config, boot) — controllers stay thin; handlers own use cases; domain has zero Spring imports.

solutionList.title: Financial correctness by design
solutionList.description: Balances derived from ledger entries (never stored), BigDecimal money, transfer state machine, loan amortization schedule, and Result<T> for domain failures.

solutionList.title: Production integrations enabled in docker profile
solutionList.description: Redis for refresh tokens, JWT blocklist, payment idempotency, and token-bucket rate limits; Kafka ingress for notification dispatch when BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true.

solutionList.title: Deployed minimalist stack on AWS
solutionList.description: Single EC2 instance runs the app container (docker/compose.yml); RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis are external; Kafka broker is a cloud instance the app consumes from; Prometheus/Grafana/Loki for observability on EC2.

solutionList.title: OpenAPI-first API surface
solutionList.description: REST under /api/v1/* with Swagger UI at /swagger-ui.html and standardized ApiResponse envelope.

metricsList: REST API v1 under /api/v1/ (auth, accounts, payments, loans, audit, notifications)
Docker image: Eclipse Temurin 21 JRE Alpine, ZGC, healthcheck on /actuator/health
Local full stack: postgres, redis, kafka, prometheus, grafana, loki, nginx via compose.local.yml

designPatterns.title: Result<T> for failures
designPatterns.description: Expected domain failures (INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS) return Result.failure instead of exceptions for control flow.

designPatterns.title: Idempotency key
designPatterns.description: Redis + DB dedup for payment mutations — safe retries from mobile/unstable networks.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API container

scalabilityStrategies.title: RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed backups and storage; connection pooling via HikariCP; Flyway migrations on deploy.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Kafka notification pipeline
scalabilityStrategies.description: Decouple notification dispatch from request thread; consumer group on cloud Kafka handles burst traffic.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Rate limiting fail-open
scalabilityStrategies.description: Redis token bucket protects auth and payments; configurable fail-open if Redis unreachable (logged, not silent).

securityStrategies.title: RS256 JWT + permissions
securityStrategies.description: Spring Security filter chain; @PreAuthorize on sensitive endpoints; permissions[] in JWT claims.

securityStrategies.title: Refresh rotation + blocklist
securityStrategies.description: Old refresh invalidated on rotate; logout adds jti to Redis blocklist for remaining access token TTL.

securityStrategies.title: Append-only audit
securityStrategies.description: DB trigger prevents audit record mutation; full event JSON in payload for reconstruction.

securityStrategies.title: Idempotency on writes
securityStrategies.description: Payments require Idempotency-Key; prevents duplicate transfers on client retries.

securityStrategies.title: CORS configuration
securityStrategies.description: bank.http.cors.allowed-origins from env — empty by default; set explicitly for frontends.

cacheStrategies.name: IAM refresh tokens
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key iam:refresh:{sha256(token)} → userId, TTL 7 days

cacheStrategies.name: JWT blocklist
cacheStrategies.description: Redis key blocklist:{jti} for revoked access tokens

cacheStrategies.name: Transfer idempotency
cacheStrategies.description: Cached Result<TransferResponse> per userId + idempotencyKey

cacheStrategies.name: Rate limit buckets
cacheStrategies.description: Lua token-bucket script — atomic refill + consume per IP/user key

techDecisions.title: Modular monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Portfolio project needs credible domain boundaries without Kubernetes operational cost.
techDecisions.solution: Maven multi-module monolith with ApplicationEvent + optional Kafka; single Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Fast iteration locally; clean extraction path documented in .agents/architecture.md.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Financial data requires ACID, relational integrity, and append-only audit with triggers.
techDecisions.solution: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations; Hibernate ddl-auto=validate.
techDecisions.outcome: Production-grade persistence; same SQL dialect in local compose.local.yml.

techDecisions.title: Upstash Redis
techDecisions.problem: JWT refresh, blocklist, idempotency, and rate limits need shared state across EC2 restarts.
techDecisions.solution: External Upstash Redis — not containerized in production compose.yml.
techDecisions.outcome: Survives container redeploys; works with multiple EC2 instances behind ALB.

techDecisions.title: Cloud Kafka for notifications
techDecisions.problem: Notification dispatch should not block HTTP threads or be lost on app restart.
techDecisions.solution: Kafka broker on cloud instance; BANK_KAFKA_ENABLED=true and dispatch-mode=kafka in docker profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Same code path testable locally via compose.local.yml kafka service.

techDecisions.title: EC2 observability stack
techDecisions.problem: Need metrics and logs without managed CloudWatch-only setup for portfolio demo.
techDecisions.solution: Prometheus scrapes app on EC2; Grafana dashboards; Loki for log aggregation; optional ELK profile.
techDecisions.outcome: Full visibility documented in docker/MONITORING.md and docs/OBSERVABILITY.md.

dockerFiles.description: Single app service — Postgres, Redis, and Kafka are external cloud instances configured via repo-root .env.
dockerFiles.content:
name: bank-api
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    image: bank-api:${BANK_API_IMAGE_TAG}
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    environment:
      SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE: docker
    ports:
      - "${APP_HTTP_PORT}:8080"
    restart: unless-stopped
# External: RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis + Cloud Kafka
# Observability: Prometheus/Grafana/Loki on EC2 (see docker/MONITORING.md)

dockerFiles.description: JDK 21 Alpine builder runs mvn package; JRE 21 Alpine runtime runs as non-root bank user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine AS builder
RUN ./mvnw -pl bank-boot -am package -DskipTests
FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
USER bank
EXPOSE 8080
ENV JAVA_OPTS="-XX:+UseZGC"
HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8080/actuator/health
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java $JAVA_OPTS -jar /app/app.jar"]

name: Cinema Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Enterprise-grade showtime and seat reservation microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Handles scheduling lifecycle, business-rule validation, catalog integration via gRPC,
Redis caching, and optional Kafka event publishing.

# catalog-service
Enterprise-grade catalog microservice for cinemas, theaters, movies, and seats.
Exposes REST and gRPC APIs with Clean Architecture, optional Kafka events,
Redis caching, and JWT role-based access control.

# concession-service
Cinema concession microservice for products, categories, combo meals, and promotional
campaigns. REST API v2 for clients; gRPC ConcessionCatalogService for peer services.
DDD/CQRS with Redis caching, JWT RBAC, and Docker-orchestrated deployment.

# employee-service
HR and workforce microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages employee records,
work schedules, and vacation requests with JWT role-based access, Redis caching,
and async PostgreSQL persistence. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis.

# notification-service
Centralized multi-channel notification microservice for the Cinema Platform.
Delivers Email (SMTP/Jinja2), SMS (Twilio), Push, and In-App notifications
via REST API and Kafka event consumption with MongoDB history and deduplication.

# payment-service
Cinema platform payment microservice — tickets, concessions, merchandise,
subscriptions, and wallet credits. Stripe gateway, PostgreSQL persistence,
Kafka events, role-split REST APIs (customer/staff/admin), and payment method catalog.

# ticket-service
Cinema ticket microservice — purchase, cancel, and use tickets; seat availability
and quotes from Mongo billboard replica; PostgreSQL ticket store; Kafka sync;
outbound gRPC to payment and billboard services.

# user-service
Cinema platform user microservice — registration, JWT sessions, 2FA (TOTP),
profiles, admin user management, Redis session store, Kafka events, and gRPC
directory lookups for other services.

# wallet-service
Digital wallet microservice for the Cinema Platform. Manages customer balances,
credit top-ups, ticket/concession payments, transaction history, and staff
reporting. Deployed on AWS EC2 with Amazon RDS and Upstash Redis; optional
Kafka event publishing and gRPC placeholder for future inter-service RPC.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Cinema Platform services.

# billboard-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized service to schedule showtimes across theaters,
track seat availability in real time, enforce booking business rules, and coordinate
with catalog and payment services. Without a dedicated billboard service, scheduling
logic leaks into multiple apps, seat state becomes inconsistent, and integrations
are brittle.

# catalog-service
Cinema platforms need a single source of truth for movies, cinema locations,
theater screens, and seat layouts. Downstream services (billboard, ticket, payment)
require fast, consistent catalog lookups — often via gRPC — while admins need
rich CRUD with business-rule enforcement and audit-friendly soft deletes.

# concession-service
Theaters need a unified backend for concession inventory — snacks, beverages,
combos, and time-limited promotions — with low-latency reads for POS systems
and integration with ticketing/loyalty microservices. Monolithic catalogs lack
flexible pricing, promotion rules, and protocol choice (REST vs gRPC).

# employee-service
Cinema chains need a dedicated service to track staff across departments (projection,
concessions, ticketing, security, etc.), manage shift schedules, and handle vacation
approvals. Without a centralized employee service, HR data fragments across spreadsheets
and ad-hoc tools, schedule conflicts go undetected, and authorization for workforce
APIs becomes inconsistent across the platform.

# notification-service
Each Cinema Platform microservice historically handled its own emails, SMS, and
in-app alerts. That leads to inconsistent templates, scattered SMTP/Twilio credentials,
duplicate deliveries on retried Kafka events, and poor operational visibility when
notifications fail.

# payment-service
Cinema purchases span tickets, concessions, merchandise, subscriptions, and wallet
top-ups across multiple services. A dedicated payment service must orchestrate
provider integration (Stripe), enforce refund rules, emit events, and expose
role-appropriate APIs while keeping PCI-sensitive data off the platform database.

# ticket-service
Cinema platforms need reliable ticket purchasing, seat reservations, and lifecycle
management while integrating with billboard showtimes and payment authorization.
High concurrency requires atomic seat handling with authoritative validation.

# user-service
Cinema platforms need centralized user management for registration, authentication,
profiles, and authorization across microservices. Legacy systems often lack secure
session management, MFA, and event-driven lifecycle notifications.

# wallet-service
Cinema platforms need a centralized wallet service so customers can preload
credit, pay for tickets and concessions, and review transaction history.
Without a dedicated wallet bounded context, payment logic leaks into order
and user services, balances become inconsistent, and financial audit trails
are incomplete.

decisions.title: FastAPI over Flask/Django
decisions.problem: Need async-capable, high-performance REST with automatic OpenAPI.
decisions.solution: FastAPI with native async, Pydantic validation, and built-in /docs.
decisions.outcome: Low-latency handlers, type-safe DTOs, self-documenting API.
alternatives: Flask with async extensions (less mature typing)

decisions.title: gRPC for Catalog Integration
decisions.problem: Low-latency service-to-service validation of movies and theaters.
decisions.solution: gRPC + protobuf CatalogGateway adapter.
decisions.outcome: Sub-30 ms catalog lookups under normal network conditions.

decisions.title: Business Rules in Domain Entity
decisions.problem: Validation logic duplicated across use cases and API layer.
decisions.solution: Centralize in Showtime.validate_business_logic() and lifecycle methods.
decisions.outcome: Testable, reusable rules independent of HTTP framework.

decisions.title: Embedded gRPC Server
decisions.problem: Peer services need fast catalog validation without HTTP overhead.
decisions.solution: Start grpc.aio.Server in FastAPI lifespan alongside REST.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; sub-30 ms lookups for movie/theater checks.

decisions.title: Multi-Package Domains
decisions.problem: Movies, cinemas, theaters have different rules but share infra.
decisions.solution: app/movies, app/cinema, app/theater packages with shared core.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; independent evolution per domain.

decisions.title: Theater Capacity in Domain
decisions.problem: Invalid capacities must be rejected before DB write.
decisions.solution: CAPACITY_RULES ClassVar on Theater entity with validate_capacity().
decisions.outcome: Testable rules; consistent errors across REST and future gRPC mutations.

decisions.title: CQRS over anemic services
decisions.problem: Read and write shapes differ (search vs create).
decisions.solution: Explicit Command/Query types per use case.
decisions.outcome: Clearer tests; optimized query objects for pagination.
alternatives: Single service class per entity (more coupling)

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Gunicorn workers not ideal for long-lived gRPC server.
decisions.solution: docker-entrypoint serve-grpc runs app.grpc.server standalone.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling and failure isolation for RPC.
alternatives: Embed gRPC in FastAPI lifespan (catalog pattern)

decisions.title: UUID value objects
decisions.problem: String IDs prone to format errors across services.
decisions.solution: ProductId/ComboId/PromotionId with from_string validation.
decisions.outcome: Consistent parsing in REST and gRPC layers.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Need managed Redis without VPC cluster ops for a portfolio-scale deployment.
decisions.solution: Use Upstash serverless Redis with TLS URL in REDIS_URL; graceful fallback if unavailable.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional so API remains functional without Redis.
alternatives: AWS ElastiCache in same VPC as EC2
In-memory cache (rejected — not shared across replicas)

decisions.title: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Durable workforce data with backups and SSL.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL with POSTGRES_SSL_MODE=require from EC2.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups, patching, and connection endpoint separate from app host.

decisions.title: EC2 + Docker Compose (prod)
decisions.problem: Simple deployment for a single-region cinema platform demo.
decisions.solution: docker-compose.prod.yml runs app container only; external RDS and Upstash via .env.
decisions.outcome: Portable image; same Dockerfile for local and cloud.

decisions.title: MongoDB over PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Notification documents vary by channel with nested content/recipient.
decisions.solution: Document store with flexible schema and Motor async driver.
decisions.outcome: Fast writes and rich filter queries without heavy joins.

decisions.title: In-process Kafka consumer
decisions.problem: Need event-driven notifications without separate worker deploy initially.
decisions.solution: asyncio tasks in FastAPI lifespan calling kafka-python poll loop.
decisions.outcome: Single deployable unit; consumer stops cleanly on shutdown.

decisions.title: Jinja2 email templates
decisions.problem: Consistent branding across notification types.
decisions.solution: Template map per NotificationType in EmailService.
decisions.outcome: HTML emails with injected template_data from content.data.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for payments
decisions.problem: ACID transactions, audit trail, and relational reporting.
decisions.solution: Async SQLAlchemy with Alembic migrations on cinema_payment DB.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for money movement and admin summaries.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Isolate long-lived gRPC server from HTTP workers.
decisions.solution: app-grpc service with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Ready for inbound RPC; servicers not registered yet.
alternatives: Embedded gRPC in same process as REST

decisions.title: Stripe as primary gateway
decisions.problem: Card payments and stored payment methods with PCI scope reduction.
decisions.solution: PaymentGateway port with Stripe adapter and token storage.
decisions.outcome: PaymentIntent flow with webhook handler on staff routes.
alternatives: PayPal / Adyen adapters (catalog supports provider enum)

decisions.title: Mongo for billboard replica
decisions.problem: Ticket reads must not synchronously call billboard on every quote.
decisions.solution: Event-synced Mongo collections from Kafka billboard.events.
decisions.outcome: Sub-50ms reads; eventual consistency acceptable for quotes.
alternatives: Synchronous HTTP to billboard on every request

decisions.title: PostgreSQL for tickets
decisions.problem: ACID seat assignment and ticket lifecycle auditing.
decisions.solution: Relational ticket and seat tables with Alembic migrations.
decisions.outcome: Strong consistency for sold seats and status transitions.
alternatives: Tickets only in Mongo (weaker transactional guarantees)

decisions.title: CQRS routers
decisions.problem: Write operations need stricter limits and different OpenAPI tags.
decisions.solution: Separate command and query APIRouter modules, same prefix.
decisions.outcome: Clear Swagger grouping; independent rate limit policies.

decisions.title: Redis for refresh tokens
decisions.problem: Need revocable sessions with logout-all support.
decisions.solution: Store refresh tokens in Redis; access tokens remain stateless JWT.
decisions.outcome: Fast invalidation without DB round-trips on every API call.

decisions.title: Integer user IDs
decisions.problem: Legacy schema and sequential admin tooling.
decisions.solution: Auto-increment integer PK on users table.
decisions.outcome: Simpler joins in ticket service; differs from UUID-based services.

decisions.title: Separate gRPC container
decisions.problem: Directory lookups should not compete with HTTP worker pool.
decisions.solution: app-grpc with serve-grpc entrypoint on port 50051.
decisions.outcome: Independent scaling; verify app.grpc module path matches sources.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over ElastiCache
decisions.problem: Managed Redis without VPC cluster administration for portfolio deployment.
decisions.solution: Upstash TLS URL in REDIS_URL from EC2 .env.
decisions.outcome: Lower ops overhead; cache optional.

decisions.title: Kafka over RabbitMQ
decisions.problem: Legacy README referenced RabbitMQ; codebase uses kafka-python.
decisions.solution: Optional Kafka publisher with noop fallback when KAFKA_ENABLED=false.
decisions.outcome: Aligns with other cinema platform services; no RabbitMQ dependency.

decisions.title: UUID wallet identifiers
decisions.problem: Distributed user-service integration needs stable external IDs.
decisions.solution: WalletId and UserId value objects wrapping UUID.
decisions.outcome: JWT sub maps directly to user_id for customer routes.

    await init_cache()

async def IsMovieActive(self, request, context):
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        repo = SQLAlchemyMovieRepository(session)
        movie = await repo.find_by_id(request.movie_id)
        return catalog_pb2.IsMovieActiveResponse(
            is_active=bool(movie and movie.is_active)
        )

codeExamples.title: Lifespan — gRPC Co-Startup
codeExamples.description: FastAPI lifespan starts gRPC server alongside REST.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: gRPC server stopped gracefully on shutdown (5s grace).
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await run_postgres_startup_check(engine)
    grpc_server = create_catalog_grpc_server()
    await grpc_server.start()
    app.state.grpc_server = grpc_server
    yield
    await grpc_server.stop(grace=5)

def mark_as_sent(self, sent_at: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.SENT
    self.sent_at = sent_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.failed_at = None
    self.error_details = None

def mark_as_failed(self, error_details: str, ...) -> None:
    self.status = NotificationStatus.FAILED
    self.failed_at = failed_at or datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    self.error_details = error_details

def mark_attention_open(self) -> None:
    self.is_important = True
    self.attention_status = NotificationAttentionStatus.OPEN

name: Company Expenses API
description: REST API for corporate expense reporting: employees submit claims with receipts, managers approve or reject, finance processes reimbursements, and admins configure company settings—with JWT security, Flyway migrations, and Docker deploy.

problemStatement.problemDescription: Companies often track employee expenses in spreadsheets or email threads. Managers lack a single queue for pending approvals, finance cannot tie reimbursements to approved claims, and employees have no audit trail for receipts or rejection reasons.
problemList: Expense status scattered across email and spreadsheets
No role-separated API for employees vs managers vs finance vs admins
Receipt attachments stored ad hoc without linkage to expense records
Managers need paginated filters by status and monthly summaries
No standardized JSON envelope for frontend clients

solution.solutionTitle: A Spring Boot API for the full expense lifecycle

solutionList.title: JWT auth with role-based routes
solutionList.description: Register employees and managers publicly; admins register other admins. OAuth2 resource server validates Bearer tokens with roles claim mapped to Spring authorities.

solutionList.title: Employee expense submission
solutionList.description: Authenticated employees create PENDING expenses, upload multipart attachments, and list their own claims via JWT subject email.

solutionList.title: Manager approval workflow
solutionList.description: Managers and admins approve or reject expenses, soft-delete records, filter by status with sorting, and fetch date-range summaries.

solutionList.title: Finance reimbursements
solutionList.description: MANAGER or FINANCE roles create reimbursements linked to expenses; notifications fire on state changes.

solutionList.title: Admin settings & dashboard
solutionList.description: Admin-only dashboard stats and mutable company settings stored in the database.

solutionList.title: Containerized local and cloud deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, local compose with PostgreSQL 15, production compose with external DB host from `.env`.

metricsList: ~75 main Java source files + 11 test classes
2 Flyway SQL migrations (schema + demo seed data)
API served on port 8080; Swagger UI at `/swagger-ui.html`
Global Bucket4j interceptor on `/**` (20 token bucket, 10 refill/min)

techDecisions.title: Spring Boot monolith

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL + Flyway

techDecisions.title: JWT (OAuth2 resource server)

codeExamples.title: Path-based security rules
codeExamples.description: SecurityConfig declares public auth/swagger routes and role requirements per API prefix.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Note FINANCIAL vs Role.FINANCE mismatch for reimbursement routes.
files.content:
.authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/auth/**", "/swagger-ui/**", "/api-docs/**").permitAll()
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/admin/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/employees/**").authenticated()
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/manager/**").hasAnyRole("MANAGER", "ADMIN")
    .requestMatchers("/v1/api/reimbursements/**").hasAnyRole("MANAGER", "FINANCIAL")
    .anyRequest().authenticated())
.oauth2ResourceServer(oauth2 -> oauth2.jwt(jwt -> jwt
    .decoder(jwtDecoder)
    .jwtAuthenticationConverter(jwtAuthenticationConverter())));

name: Cooking Recipes API
description: REST API for a cooking-recipes platform: user accounts with JWT sessions, recipe CRUD, advanced search, favorites, reviews, and view analytics—built with Clean Architecture and deployable on AWS.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Scattered recipe data and weak discovery
problemStatement.problemDescription: Home cooks and food bloggers need one backend to publish structured recipes (ingredients, steps, nutrition), let users search and filter by cuisine or diet, save favorites, and leave ratings—without coupling the UI to SQLite files or ad-hoc JSON.
problemList: Recipe content lives in spreadsheets or static JSON with no auth or ownership
Search cannot combine filters (cuisine, meal type, cooking time, rating) efficiently
No session model for multi-device login and secure logout
Favorites and reviews duplicated across clients with no single source of truth
No production path from local SQLite to managed PostgreSQL on AWS

solution.solutionTitle: A FastAPI backend with DDD modules

solutionList.title: Auth module with JWT + Redis sessions
solutionList.description: Signup/login, refresh tokens stored in Redis, logout with optional revoke-all-devices; bcrypt password hashing.

solutionList.title: Recipe module with use cases
solutionList.description: Create/update/soft-delete recipes, specification-based search, featured list, view counter, favorites toggle, and reviews.

solutionList.title: Clean Architecture boundaries
solutionList.description: Domain entities and repository interfaces; application use cases; SQLAlchemy and Redis in infrastructure; FastAPI controllers in presentation.

solutionList.title: Operational readiness
solutionList.description: Docker image, health checks, Alembic migrations via start.sh, rate limiting, CORS for Angular frontend on :4200.

solutionList.title: AWS-ready deploy
solutionList.description: Documented path to ECS + RDS + ElastiCache; local docker-compose bundles Redis; production uses external managed services.

techDecisions.problem: Simple local dev; production needs managed RDS.

techDecisions.outcome: Secure logout; refresh fails with 404 if session removed.

codeExamples.title: Application lifespan (DB + Redis)
codeExamples.description: main.py wires async startup/shutdown for database and Redis used by auth sessions.

files.name: main.py
files.explanation: ECS tasks rely on successful Redis connect before accepting traffic if health checks include dependency probes.
files.content:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await init_db()
    await initialize_redis()
    yield
    await close_redis()
    await close_db()

app = FastAPI(
    title="Cooking Recipes API",
    version="1.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

FROM python:3.11-slim

COPY . .
EXPOSE 8080 8443

CMD ["./start.sh"]

problemStatement.problemTitle: Unified CRM and marketing data without a monolithic frontend lock-in

decisions.title: GraphQL over REST
decisions.problem: Multiple CRM and marketing clients need flexible reads without version explosion.
decisions.solution: Single GraphQL schema with modular SDL and Spring GraphQL controllers.
alternatives: OpenAPI REST with many DTO permutations
decisions.outcome: One endpoint, strong typing, GraphiQL for developer experience.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules per domain
decisions.problem: Marketing and CRM logic must stay testable as the system grows.
decisions.solution: package-by-domain with explicit ports (input/output) and adapters.
decisions.outcome: Clear boundaries; swap GraphQL or JPA without touching domain rules.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis instead of self-hosted on EC2
decisions.problem: Session and rate-limit state must survive app restarts and scale horizontally.
decisions.solution: Managed Upstash Redis with TLS; configured via SPRING_REDIS_HOST in production.
decisions.outcome: No Redis ops on EC2; suitable for minimalist deployment.

decisions.title: AWS RDS for PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Need durable relational storage with backups for CRM/marketing data.
decisions.solution: RDS PostgreSQL 16; Flyway migrations on startup.
decisions.outcome: Managed backups and patching; app connects via JDBC env vars.

name: MindCare Hub API
description: REST API for small mental-health practices: patient onboarding, specialist scheduling, confidential medical records, billing, and notifications for psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented tools for small therapy practices
problemStatement.problemDescription: Independent psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists often juggle spreadsheets, paper notes, separate calendars, and ad-hoc billing. That creates scheduling conflicts, weak access control on sensitive clinical data, and no single view of a patient's care journey.
problemList: Double bookings and unclear specialist availability across the week
Medical notes stored without role-based confidentiality rules
Patients, staff, and specialists using different systems for appointments and invoices
Manual follow-up for activation emails, reminders, and payment status
No unified API for a future patient portal or clinic admin dashboard

solution.solutionTitle: One API built for mental-health workflows

solutionList.title: Role-based access for the whole clinic
solutionList.description: JWT authentication with user types (patient, specialist, staff, admin) and service-layer checks on appointments, records, and payments.

solutionList.title: Scheduling that respects clinic rules
solutionList.description: Appointments enforce clinic hours (09:00–19:00), advance booking windows, overlap detection, and specialist daily caps.

solutionList.title: Confidential medical records
solutionList.description: Records support standard, sensitive, and highly_sensitive levels; patients cannot read highly sensitive entries; specialists own their documentation.

solutionList.title: Billing aligned with real practices
solutionList.description: Bills, cash and bank-transfer payments, Stripe online payments, refunds, and insurance claims in one billing module.

solutionList.title: Production-ready container deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, Gunicorn, health checks, and external PostgreSQL plus Redis for cache, rate limits, and Celery.

metricsList: 6 Django domain apps (users, specialists, appointments, medical, billing, notification)
REST API v2 under /api/v2/ (appointments router mounted at /appointments/)
129+ Python modules in apps/ excluding migrations and tests
Docker image Python 3.11 slim, Gunicorn 3 workers × 2 threads

techDecisions.title: Django monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Small therapy group needs fast delivery and simple ops, not distributed complexity.
techDecisions.solution: Single Django codebase with clear app boundaries and service classes; one Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Lower operational overhead; scale vertically and with extra EC2 instances first.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Relational data with strict integrity for appointments, records, and billing.
techDecisions.solution: Managed RDS PostgreSQL with connection pooling (CONN_MAX_AGE 600).
techDecisions.outcome: ACID transactions for clinical and financial data; dev uses SQLite in development.py.

techDecisions.title: SimpleJWT
techDecisions.problem: SPA/mobile clients need stateless auth for patients and specialists.
techDecisions.solution: JWT access + refresh with rotation; custom claims for user_type.
techDecisions.outcome: Works well for API-first clients; requires blacklist app for secure logout.

techDecisions.title: External Redis (Upstash)
techDecisions.problem: Rate limits and cache should survive API container restarts on EC2.
techDecisions.solution: django-redis to cloud Redis; compose file does not bundle Redis container.
techDecisions.outcome: Shared cache across multiple API instances behind ALB.

dockerFiles.description: Python 3.11 slim bookworm; builder installs requirements; runtime runs as non-root django user.

FROM python:3.11-slim-bookworm AS builder
# gcc, libpq-dev, pip install -r requirements.txt

EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]
# Gunicorn: 3 workers, 2 threads, migrate on start

dockerFiles.description: Waits for DB_HOST/REDIS_HOST, runs migrate, optional superuser/dummy data, starts Gunicorn.

# nc -z REDIS_HOST REDIS_PORT  (Redis ready)
python manage.py migrate --noinput
gunicorn config.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000} --workers 3 --threads 2

name: Pimienta Alimentos Backend
description: Production Spring Boot API for Pimienta Alimentos — a real-world company operations platform used daily by employees and staff. Covers HR, payroll, inventory, CRM, contracts, tasks, and file management.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented operations for a growing food company
problemStatement.problemDescription: Pimienta Alimentos needed a single backend to coordinate employees, payroll, inventory, sales opportunities, contracts, and internal tasks across headquarters — replacing spreadsheets, ad-hoc tools, and disconnected processes that did not scale with daily staff operations.
problemList: Employee records, attendance, and work schedules lived in separate spreadsheets with no audit trail
Inventory movements (purchase, sale, transfer) lacked approval workflows and real-time stock visibility
Payroll periods and adjustments were error-prone without a centralized ledger
CRM opportunities and internal projects had no shared pipeline or milestone tracking
No secure, role-based API for the company frontend used by staff in the field and office
Bulk data updates (headquarters, tasks, payroll) required manual re-entry

solutionList.title: Modular bounded contexts
solutionList.description: Ten domain modules (account, employees, contract, CRM, task, headquarter, inventory, payroll, files, notification) each follow ports-and-adapters architecture.

solutionList.title: JWT authentication with admin approval
solutionList.description: Staff register and log in via JWT; new accounts require administrator approval before access. Refresh tokens stored in Redis (Upstash in production).

solutionList.title: Operational workflows
solutionList.description: Inventory transactions, contract renewals, opportunity pipeline (win/lose/reopen), attendance start/end workday, and payroll adjustments are first-class API workflows.

solutionList.title: Bulk XLSX import/export
solutionList.description: Apache POI powers spreadsheet sync for employees, opportunities, tasks, headquarters, and payroll — matching how operations teams already work.

solutionList.title: AWS-backed file storage
solutionList.description: Employee photos and file assets upload to S3 with presigned download URLs and server-side image resizing.

solutionList.title: Production-ready infrastructure
solutionList.description: Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis; Docker images built via multi-stage Maven pipeline.

decisions.title: Hexagonal modules over layered monolith
decisions.problem: Ten operational domains (HR, inventory, payroll, CRM) would tangle in a single package.
decisions.solution: Bounded contexts under module/* with core + adapter split and shared kernel (BaseDomain, pagination).
alternatives: Single-layered Spring package by technical concern
decisions.outcome: Clear ownership per domain; deployable monolith suitable for current team size and EC2 footprint.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis over in-container Redis
decisions.problem: Production on EC2 needs durable cache/session store without co-locating Redis on the same VM.
decisions.solution: REDIS_URL with rediss:// to Upstash; key prefix for multi-tenant safety.
decisions.outcome: Managed TLS Redis for refresh tokens and rate limits with minimal ops overhead.

decisions.title: Flyway + validate ddl-auto
decisions.problem: Schema drift between environments risks production data corruption.
decisions.solution: Versioned migrations in db/migration; Hibernate validates entity mapping only.
decisions.outcome: Reproducible schema across local Docker, CI, and RDS.

decisions.title: JWT stateless sessions
decisions.problem: Staff clients need mobile-friendly auth without server-side HTTP sessions.
decisions.solution: Short-lived access JWT + Redis-backed refresh tokens; stateless SecurityFilterChain.
decisions.outcome: Scalable auth model aligned with SPA/frontend clients.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Central security policy for the production API used by all staff clients.
files.content:
http.csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
    .sessionManagement(s -> s.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
    .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
        .requestMatchers("/api/v1/auth/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/api/v2/health/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/api/v1/files/management/**").hasRole("ADMIN")
        .anyRequest().authenticated())
    .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthenticationFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage build from backend project root; non-root user; exposes 8080.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-25-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /workspace
COPY pom.xml .
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn -q -B -DskipTests package

FROM eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
RUN addgroup -S spring -g 1001 && adduser -S spring -u 1001 -G spring
COPY --from=build /workspace/target/*.jar /app/app.jar
RUN mkdir -p /app/logs && chown -R spring:spring /app
USER spring:spring
EXPOSE 8080
ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=docker
ENTRYPOINT ["sh", "-c", "exec java ${JAVA_OPTS:-} -jar /app/app.jar"]

name: Restaurant Management API
description: Production REST API for restaurant operations — JWT auth, menu and stock, table seating, orders, payments with 16% VAT, and reservations. Deployed with AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis (via REDIS_URL).

problemStatement.problemDescription: Small and mid-size restaurants often track menus, table availability, orders, inventory, and payments in separate tools or spreadsheets. That leads to double-booked tables, stock overselling, inconsistent billing, and no single API for a POS or admin dashboard.
problemList: Tables marked free while an active order is in progress
Menu changes not reflected consistently across ordering channels
Manual VAT and payment totals prone to error
Stock depletion discovered only after orders are placed
Reservation requests handled outside the order/table system

solution.solutionTitle: One API for front-of-house and back-of-house

solutionList.title: JWT-authenticated staff workflows
solutionList.description: Signup, login, logout, and logout-all via SimpleJWT with standardized JSON responses and 8-hour access tokens.

solutionList.title: Table-aware ordering
solutionList.description: Starting an order atomically marks the table unavailable; completing or cancelling releases it.

solutionList.title: Menu with cache-backed categories
solutionList.description: Public dish browsing with Upstash Redis-cached category lists and 15-minute HTTP cache on dish detail.

solutionList.title: Payments aligned to orders
solutionList.description: Payment records auto-created on order completion with 16% VAT via PaymentCalculatorService.

solutionList.title: Reservations with auto table assignment
solutionList.description: Customer reservation requests validate date, party size (max 8), and assign a suitable table when possible.

solutionList.title: Deployed cloud stack
solutionList.description: Dockerized Django API connected to AWS RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis for production cache.

techDecisions.title: AWS RDS + Upstash Redis (external managed services)
techDecisions.problem: Database and cache must survive API container restarts and scale independently.
techDecisions.solution: PostgreSQL via DJANGO_DB_* pointing to AWS RDS; cache via REDIS_URL to Upstash Redis DB 1.
alternatives: In-container PostgreSQL and Redis (docker-compose local only)
techDecisions.outcome: Production deploy uses managed RDS and Upstash; local dev uses docker-compose services.

techDecisions.title: Service layer over fat views
techDecisions.problem: Order/table/payment rules span multiple models and need transactional integrity.
techDecisions.solution: Dedicated *Service classes with atomic blocks called from thin views.
techDecisions.outcome: Testable business logic; clear separation between HTTP and domain rules.

techDecisions.title: django-redis over Memcached
techDecisions.problem: Need a shared cache compatible with cloud Redis and local docker-compose.
techDecisions.solution: Migrated to django-redis with REDIS_URL env var (Upstash in production).
techDecisions.outcome: Single REDIS_URL config works for Upstash, local Redis, and docker-compose.

)

dockerFiles.description: Production-ready API image — Python 3.12-slim, libpq-dev, installs requirements.txt, exposes 8000.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /restaurant_management_backend
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y libpq-dev gcc
COPY requirements.txt /restaurant_management_backend/
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . /restaurant_management_backend/
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python3", "manage.py", "runserver", "0.0.0.0:8000"]

    ports: ["8000:8000"]

      - DJANGO_DB_USER=postgres
      - DJANGO_DB_PASSWORD=postgres

name: Social Events API
description: REST API for discovering, hosting, and attending social events—organizations, ticket tiers, registrations, comments, in-app notifications, OAuth/JWT auth, and Celery-backed async work. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented event management for communities and organizers
problemStatement.problemDescription: Community groups, venues, and independent organizers often rely on spreadsheets, generic form tools, and disconnected chat apps to publish events, sell or assign tickets, and keep attendees informed. That leads to double bookings, weak access control, no audit trail, and poor engagement after signup.
problemList: No single API for organizations to own events, members, and branding
Manual registration lists without capacity, waitlist, or check-in flows
Comments and notifications scattered outside the event domain
Authentication friction across web and mobile clients
Difficulty scaling traffic without caching and background email delivery

solution.solutionTitle: One API for the full event lifecycle

solutionList.title: Multi-tenant organizations
solutionList.description: Organizations with memberships, invites, join/leave, and org-scoped event listings for clubs and venues.

solutionList.title: Rich event domain
solutionList.description: Draft/publish/cancel, categories, tags, recurrence, staff roles (host, co-host, moderator), favorites, and personalized feeds.

solutionList.title: Registrations & ticketing
solutionList.description: Ticket tiers with pricing and capacity; registrations with pending, confirmed, waitlist, cancelled, attended states and host check-in.

solutionList.title: Engagement layer
solutionList.description: Threaded comments with likes and host pin/unpin; in-app notifications with read state and email templates.

solutionList.title: Cloud-ready operations
solutionList.description: Docker on AWS EC2, RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis with project key prefix, optional S3 media, Nginx TLS, Celery workers.

metricsList: 7 domain Django apps (users, organizations, events, locations, registrations, comments, notifications)
85+ HTTP operations including ViewSet actions and custom routes
Redis namespace social-events-api: for cache and Celery broker
JWT access 60 min / refresh 7 days with rotation and blacklist

decisions.title: Django monolith on EC2
decisions.problem: Need rapid feature delivery for event MVP without Kubernetes complexity.
decisions.solution: Single repo, Docker image, Gunicorn on EC2; domain split into Django apps.
decisions.outcome: Simple deploy path documented in docker/README.md; scale EC2/RDS vertically first.

decisions.title: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Relational model for organizations, events, registrations, and foreign keys.
decisions.solution: RDS in production; SQLite only in config.settings.development for local runserver.
decisions.outcome: CONN_MAX_AGE 600 reduces connection overhead from Docker web container.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis with key prefix
decisions.problem: One Redis account shared across multiple portfolio projects on Upstash.
decisions.solution: REDIS_KEY_PREFIX and Celery global_keyprefix social-events-api:.
decisions.outcome: No key collisions; rediss:// for TLS to Upstash endpoint.

decisions.title: SimpleJWT + allauth
decisions.problem: SPA needs stateless auth; users want Google/GitHub sign-in.
decisions.solution: JWT for API; allauth stores social accounts; custom views return tokens.
decisions.outcome: Consistent Bearer flow; blacklist on logout when token_blacklist enabled.

decisions.title: drf-spectacular for API contract
decisions.problem: Frontend and portfolio need discoverable endpoint documentation.
decisions.solution: Schema at /api/schema/, Swagger UI at /api/docs/.
decisions.outcome: Schema stays in sync with ViewSet decorators.

name: Tourist Places API
description: REST API for discovering and managing tourist destinations: countries, categories, places, activities, reviews, and user-curated place lists—with JWT auth, Google OAuth2, and PostgreSQL on AWS RDS.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Scattered travel data with no unified API
problemStatement.problemDescription: Travel apps and content sites need a single backend to browse countries, categorize destinations, read reviews, and let users save personal place lists. Without a structured API, frontends duplicate validation, auth, and rating logic.
problemList: No shared catalog of countries, tourist places, categories, and activities
User-generated reviews and ratings stored inconsistently across clients
Account activation, password reset, and OAuth flows reimplemented per app
Hard to deploy consistently across environments without container + migration strategy
Public browse vs authenticated write operations need clear security boundaries

solution.solutionTitle: Modular Spring Boot REST API for tourist content

solutionList.title: JWT + Google OAuth2 authentication

codeExamples.description: Defines public auth/swagger routes, permissive GET access, and JWT filter placement.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: CSRF disabled; stateless sessions; OAuth2 login for Google; review GET permitAll implications before prod.
files.content:
http.csrf(AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable)
    .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
        .requestMatchers("/signup", "/login", "/auth/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers("/v3/api-docs/**", "/swagger-ui/**").permitAll()
        .requestMatchers(HttpMethod.GET, "/**").permitAll()
        .anyRequest().authenticated())
    .sessionManagement(s -> s.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
    .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);

FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS build

COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B
COPY src ./src

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy

COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

name: Veterinary Clinic API
description: REST API for veterinary clinic operations—customer and pet onboarding, staff scheduling, clinical sessions (vaccinations, prescriptions, surgeries), payments, and multi-channel notifications. Built with hexagonal architecture, PostgreSQL (sqlc), and Redis-backed JWT sessions.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Disconnected tools for small and mid-size vet clinics
problemStatement.problemDescription: Veterinary practices often run reception desks, clinical records, pet owner portals, and billing on separate spreadsheets or legacy software. That leads to double bookings, lost vaccination history, weak access control on pet medical data, and no single API for a future mobile app or admin dashboard.
problemList: Staff cannot see a unified schedule across veterinarians and receptionists
Pet owners lack self-service booking and read-only access to their pets' clinical history
Clinical data (sessions, vaccines, prescriptions) scattered across paper or ad-hoc files
Payments and appointment status not linked in one transactional system
No standardized REST contract for integrators or a future client portal

solution.solutionTitle: One Go API for end-to-end clinic workflows

solutionList.title: JWT auth with role-aware routes
solutionList.description: Register, activate, login, refresh, logout, 2FA hooks, and password reset with tokens stored in Redis; Gin middleware enforces Bearer JWT on protected routes.

solutionList.title: Appointments with domain rules
solutionList.description: Customers request visits; employees manage assigned appointments; admins/managers search and operate clinic-wide—overlap detection (30 min buffer) and hourly capacity (max 5 concurrent).

solutionList.title: Customers, employees, and pets
solutionList.description: CRUD for clinic customers and staff profiles; pet records tied to customers with species/gender validation and soft-delete restore flows.

solutionList.title: Medical sessions (clinical module)
solutionList.description: Sessions with vaccinations, surgeries, prescriptions, attachments, services, and catalogs—customer read-only `/me/medical` vs staff write `/api/v2/medical` (service wiring in progress).

solutionList.title: Payments and notifications
solutionList.description: Payment lifecycle (create, process, cancel, refund) and notification inbox plus staff send/summary endpoints; email/SMS senders pluggable via env.

solutionList.title: Container-ready deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, golang-migrate on startup, local full stack via `./docker/up-local.sh`, dev profile against cloud RDS + Upstash.

techDecisions.title: Go monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Solo/small team needs one deployable unit and strong typing without network chatter between services.
techDecisions.solution: Single module clinic-vet-api with hexagonal internal packages; scale ECS tasks horizontally.
techDecisions.outcome: Faster iteration; sqlc keeps SQL explicit and reviewable.

techDecisions.title: sqlc + pgx instead of ORM
techDecisions.problem: Complex clinical queries and performance predictability for appointment search.
techDecisions.solution: SQL files in db/queries generate type-safe Go; migrations in db/migrations.
techDecisions.outcome: Compile-time query checks; easy RDS tuning with raw SQL.

techDecisions.title: Gin over net/http alone
techDecisions.problem: Need routing groups, middleware, and fast JSON binding.
techDecisions.solution: Gin with validator integration and swagger-friendly handler structure.
techDecisions.outcome: Familiar ecosystem; middleware matches portfolio docs patterns.

techDecisions.title: Upstash Redis for tokens
techDecisions.problem: Multiple API replicas must share token revocation state.
techDecisions.solution: REDIS_URL with TLS (rediss://) for TokenService; local Redis in compose.local.
techDecisions.outcome: Matches serverless-friendly dev/prod parity; ElastiCache optional later in VPC.

techDecisions.title: Docker multi-stage Alpine

codeExamples.title: Migrate-then-run container entrypoint
codeExamples.description: Production-friendly startup: wait for Postgres, run golang-migrate up, then exec the API binary.

files.name: entrypoint.sh
files.explanation: SKIP_MIGRATIONS and SKIP_DB_WAIT escape hatches for cloud dev profile.
files.content:
. /scripts/parse-database-url.sh
until pg_isready -h "$DB_HOST" -p "$DB_PORT" -U "$DB_USER"; do sleep 2; done
migrate -path ./db/migrations -database "$DATABASE_URL" up
exec "$@"

dockerFiles.description: Postgres 12, Redis 7, and API on vet_network. Started via ./docker/up-local.sh which rewrites DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL hostnames for in-network DNS.
dockerFiles.content:
name: vet-clinic-local
services:
  postgres12:
    image: postgres:12-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD}
  api:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile }
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: ${DOCKER_DATABASE_URL}
      REDIS_URL: ${DOCKER_REDIS_URL}
    depends_on: [postgres12, redis]

dockerFiles.description: Single API container pointing at cloud RDS and Upstash; SKIP_MIGRATIONS and SKIP_DB_WAIT default true for faster dev iteration.
Builder: Go 1.24 Alpine, compile main, install migrate CLI. Runtime: Alpine 3.18, non-root /root, EXPOSE 8000, entrypoint runs migrations then ./main.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM golang:1.24.0-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download && CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o main .
FROM alpine:3.18
COPY --from=builder /app/main .
COPY db/migrations ./db/migrations/
COPY scripts/entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["./main"]
